title: Set and defend Core Web Vitals budgets
description: Hold LCP, CLS, and INP to explicit field-aligned thresholds and treat a regression past budget as a release blocker.
body: Use when changing rendering, layout, or interaction-heavy code on a user-facing route. Do not optimize a vital in isolation while regressing another, and do not judge by lab numbers alone. Actions: set targets (LCP <=2.5s, CLS <=0.1, INP <=200ms), measure before and after on a representative viewport and throttled network, and attribute any regression to the change. Validation signals include a Lighthouse/field comparison showing each vital within budget and a trace explaining any movement. Risk boundary: shipping a vitals regression degrades real-user experience and ranking; escalate to roll back if a budget is breached without justification.
